note: Private mutation/regression corpus for SwiftInferProperties — the inference engine dogfooding on itself (Chapter 30 §30.4.4). Mutants live in the IdempotenceWitnessDetector's own name-classifier; each is killed by that detector's own unit tests, which pin both what it should and should not match. Run via `swift test --filter`. Not a scored benchmark.

mutants.label: drop `reset` from the exact-name set — a genuinely idempotent action is no longer recognized as a witness
add `increment` to the exact-name set — a non-idempotent action is falsely classified as an idempotence witness
drop the `set` prefix — `setX`-style assignments are no longer recognized as prefix-match witnesses
